mission.title: linkerd: Linkerd proxy gRPC connections to unmeshed services result in `TOO MANY PINGs` with specific keepalive settings
mission.description: Linkerd proxy gRPC connections to unmeshed services result in `TOO MANY PINGs` with specific keepalive settings. Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current linkerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your linkerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
helm list -n linkerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working linkerd installation.

steps.title: Review linkerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
### What is the issue?

We recently upgraded to linkerd stable-2.10 and discovered that some unmeshed applications would have connection disconnects after about 10 seconds. The general problem would present itself as:
1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Linkerd proxy gRPC connections to unmeshed services result
steps.description:
Some HTTP/2 services are very finicky and particular about
the timing of pings. In particular, Go's implementation of gRPC
requires that clients and servers exactly match up the timing of their
keepalive pings. If pings are off for more than MAX_PING_STRIKES pings,
the connection will be closed via
```yaml
2021-04-01T16:46:05.991-04:00 | ERROR: 2021/04/01 16:46:05 transport: Got too many pings from the client, closing the connection.
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Linkerd proxy gRPC connections to unmeshed services result" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Some HTTP/2 services are very finicky and particular about
the timing of pings. In particular, Go's implementation of gRPC
requires that clients and servers exactly match up the timing of their
keepalive pings. If pings are off for more than MAX_PING_STRIKES pings,
the connection will be closed via ENHANCE YOUR CALM.
codeSnippets: 2021-04-01T16:46:05.991-04:00 | ERROR: 2021/04/01 16:46:05 transport: Got too many pings from the client, closing the connection.

- name: LINKERD2_PROXY_INBOUND_ACCEPT_KEEPALIVE
  value: 10000ms
- name: LINKERD2_PROXY_OUTBOUND_CONNECT_KEEPALIVE
  value: 10000ms

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
